top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 83, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Firenze's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Firenze. Train it until it feels boring.
Gran Fondo Firenze's paved — Italian provincial and urban roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 83 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,562ft of climbing on paved — Italian provincial and urban roads does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 83 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Gran Fondo Firenze, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 83 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved — Italian provincial and urban roads at tempo for 83 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Tempo is Gran Fondo Firenze's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 83 miles on paved — Italian provincial and urban roads. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Firenze’s 83 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 83 miles of paved — italian provincial and urban roads in Florence, Tuscany, Italy.
